brief.headline: Dashboard viability review
brief.next_action: Keep the dashboard static and evidence-backed before attempting live orchestration.
brief.attention_needed: Do not build live remote control before GitHub queue handoff is reliable.
brief.critique: A dashboard is still worth keeping as Mission Control, but only as a thin read-only cockpit. Full live orchestration is premature until GitHub comments, issue queues, and branch handoffs are consistently reliable.

failure_lessons: The dashboard should not become the source of truth; GitHub issues, commits, and comments must remain durable state.
A cockpit fails when it requires the CEO to update the same status in multiple places.
Remote approval/control should be deferred until the read-only monitoring loop is boring and reliable.
next_prompts: Update site/data.json from the latest GitHub queue evidence. Scope: site/data.json only.
Only add live sync after proving one read-only sync path from GitHub evidence.
